steps.title: Connect persistence to function
steps.content: The appendix has emerged independently in multiple mammal lineages and has not disappeared after emerging. The team also found that species with an appendix tend to have more lymphoid tissue, which supports immune responses.

steps.title: Infer the likely benefit
steps.content: Choice A shifts to nonmammals, Choice C predicts future emergence, and Choice D compares past and present tissue levels without support. The evidence supports the idea that the appendix persists in certain mammals because it benefits immune systems. The answer is B.
